sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Neurocode Launches Groundbreaking pTau217 Blood Test for Alzheimer's Disease Clinical Diagnosis
feed.summary: Neurocode USA, Inc. has launched ALZpath Dx, an innovative blood test for Alzheimer's disease (AD) that measures circulating levels of pTau217. This test is the first of its kind available for clinical diagnostic use in the U.S., offering accuracy comparable to brain imaging or CSF testing but with greater accessibility, lower cost, and less invasiveness. The ALZpath Dx test, powered by Quanterix Simoa technology, marks a significant advancement in early and accurate AD diagnosis, patient stratification for clinical trials, and treatment monitoring.

feed.title: Microsoft acquires 124 acres in Virginia’s Prince William County for $465.5m
feed.summary: Microsoft has acquired 124 acres of land in Gainesville, Prince William County, Virginia, for $465.5 million from local landowner Chuck Kuhn. The vacant plots are located in a Data Center Opportunity Zone Overlay District, with a pending rezoning application aiming for increased data center density and a new substation. This acquisition adds to Microsoft's existing landholdings in the area, which is also a hub for data center development by companies like Amazon and a Google-linked shell company.

feed.title: CEFS Stock Fund Price and Chart — CBOE:CEFS
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth overview of the Saba Closed-End Funds ETF (CEFS), traded on Cboe. It details the fund's price performance, key statistics like AUM and dividend yield, and its active management strategy which invests in a diverse group of closed-end funds. The article also includes a comprehensive FAQ section addressing common inquiries about ETFs, CEFS's pricing, NAV, expense ratio, and investment strategy.

feed.title: CAML Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:CAML
feed.summary: The CAML Congress Large Cap Growth ETF, traded on NYSE American, actively manages a portfolio of domestic large-cap growth stocks. The fund, with $334.47 million in assets under management and an expense ratio of 0.65%, focuses on high-quality companies with above-average earnings growth and free cash flows. As of the article's data, its price has fallen 1.50% in the past 24 hours, but increased 6.23% over the last year.

feed.title: BSR Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:BSR
feed.summary: BSR (Beacon Selective Risk ETF) aims to capitalize on bullish US equity markets using an active management style and a proprietary loss-reduction strategy at the sector level. The fund, with $40.17 million in assets under management, diversifies investments across 11 Vanguard sector ETFs and shifts to fixed-income ETFs during bearish trends. It has an expense ratio of 1.43% and its technical analysis currently indicates a strong buy signal.

feed.title: Netflix Director Hastings Reed Sells Over $39 Million in Company Stock
feed.summary: Netflix Director Reed Hastings sold over $39 million in company stock across three transactions on March 2, 2026. Following these sales, Hastings retains significant indirect ownership through a family trust. On the same day, Netflix CFO Spencer Adam Neumann also sold over $2.7 million in shares under a pre-arranged trading plan.

feed.title: Cal-Maine Foods acquires Creighton Brothers
feed.summary: Cal-Maine Foods, a major U.S. egg company, has acquired Creighton Brothers and Crystal Lake for $128.5 million. This acquisition significantly expands Cal-Maine Foods' shell egg platform, including both specialty and conventional eggs, adding substantial growth to its portfolio and strengthening its production capacity. The deal also enhances the company's internal sourcing strategy for its prepared foods business and adds key assets like commercial shell egg production, a feed mill, and an egg processing facility.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says "Lockheed Martin Is Still a Winner, Even Up Here"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Lockheed Martin (LMT) as a strong investment due to increased global defense spending and the company's excellent quarterly performance and forecast. He praised CEO Jim Taiclet and the company's prospects, despite acknowledging geopolitical complexities. The article also briefly promotes AI stocks as potentially offering greater upside than LMT.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Capital One: "Stick With It"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Capital One (NYSE: COF), advising investors to "stick with it" despite concerns over potential interest rate caps and the impact of AI on credit cards. He believes Capital One's internal strength, led by Richard Fairbank and its integration with Discover, is key. Cramer also noted that credit conditions are better than previously thought.

feed.title: Verato to Showcase Identity Intelligence at Gartner Data & Analytics Summit 2026
feed.summary: Verato announced it will present its identity intelligence solutions and demonstrate Verato MDM Cloud at the Gartner Data & Analytics Summit 2026 in Orlando, Florida. The company aims to highlight how identity intelligence is crucial for effective AI adoption, advanced analytics, and enterprise Customer 360 strategies, particularly in healthcare. Verato will host thought leadership sessions, including a roundtable on healthcare AI and a joint session with Snowflake, and will showcase live demos of its platform.

feed.title: Vistra Corp has reported full-year 2025 financial results
feed.summary: Vistra Corp announced its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results, reporting significant revenue growth and Adjusted EBITDA. The company saw operating revenue reach $17,738 million for FY2025, up from $17,224 million in FY2024, with Adjusted EBITDA for ongoing operations climbing to $5,912 million. Vistra also made operational advancements, including the commissioning of a new solar facility and progress on other solar projects, and projects an Adjusted EBITDA between $6,800 million and $7,600 million for 2026.

feed.title: PayPal Holdings (PYPL) Securities Fraud Class Action – April 20, 2026 Lead Plaintiff Deadline - Faruqi & Faruqi LLP
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is investigating potential securities fraud claims against PayPal Holdings (PYPL) and reminds investors of the April 20, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline. The class action alleges that PayPal made misleading statements about its salesforce's ability to execute on growth potential, leading to artificially inflated stock prices. This investigation follows a significant drop in PayPal's stock price after weaker-than-expected Q4 2025 financial results and the departure of its CEO.

feed.title: Church & Dwight (CHD) CFO awarded stock options and RSUs in new grant
feed.summary: Church & Dwight's CFO, Lee B. McChesney, has been granted stock options for 32,820 shares and 1,820 restricted stock units (RSUs) at $103.95 per share. These RSUs will vest in three equal annual installments starting March 2, 2027, as part of his executive compensation package. The transactions are grant acquisitions and not open-market buys or sells.

feed.title: Financial Planning Firm Achieves IT Excellence
feed.summary: CBIZ partnered with a leading wealth management firm to enhance its IT experience, reducing ticket resolution times and providing strategic technical direction. Through an extensive RFP process, CBIZ was selected for its customer service, technical expertise, and financial services industry familiarity. The collaboration led to increased productivity, improved customer support, a stabilized IT environment using Azure cloud, enhanced endpoint management, and efficient issue resolution, ultimately allowing the firm to focus more on client service.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Notes "Keysight's Found a Big Business With AI Data Centers"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Keysight Technologies (NYSE: KEYS) as a top performer in the S&P 500, citing its significant business in AI data centers. The company, which provides electronic design automation software and test hardware, recently reported strong quarterly results and guidance. While market conditions for Keysight have been subdued, they are now improving, and Cramer believes the company is well-positioned in the AI sector.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on DaVita: "Haven't Seen That Stock Do That Well In This Long"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted DaVita Inc.'s strong performance in February, where the stock rallied 43% after a "terrible year" in 2025. He attributed this surge to overcoming overly negative analyst and investor sentiment following a robust quarterly report. Cramer suggested that the stock's long period of underperformance made its recent rally particularly notable.

feed.title: Allivet. Petsense by Tractor Supply Form National Pet Expert Advisory Council
feed.summary: Petsense by Tractor Supply and Allivet by Tractor Supply have created a national Pet Expert Advisory Council composed of leading veterinarians and pet wellness experts. This council aims to elevate animal care standards, education, and the approach to total pet wellness, particularly as pet ownership expands beyond traditional dogs and cats to شامل birds, reptiles, fish, and small mammals. The initiative underscores the companies' commitment to providing dependable resources and veterinary excellence for all pets and their owners.

feed.title: Church & Dwight (CHD) CEO receives major stock option and RSU awards
feed.summary: Church & Dwight (CHD) President and CEO Richard A. Dierker was granted significant stock awards on March 2, 2026. These awards included stock options for 130,430 shares and 7,220 restricted stock units (RSUs) at a reference price of $103.95 per share. The RSUs will vest in three equal annual installments starting March 2, 2027, and Dierker's direct common stock holdings now total 38,658.846 shares, with additional indirect ownership.

feed.title: Cantor Fitzgerald reiterates HCA Healthcare stock Overweight rating
feed.summary: Cantor Fitzgerald has reiterated an Overweight rating on HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) stock, setting a price target of $588.00. The firm highlighted HCA's attractive valuation, strong EBITDA growth exceeding expectations, and positive analyst revisions, despite potential near-term headwinds like hurricane recovery costs and expiring Affordable Care Act tax credits. Recent Q4 2025 earnings also surpassed EPS expectations, further contributing to analyst optimism.

feed.title: S&P Global February Final Services PMI Revised Downward, Below January Index
feed.summary: The S&P Global February Final Services PMI has been revised downward, indicating a weaker performance compared to the January index. This news comes alongside an unrelated report on Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results, which showed increased revenue but a quarterly loss per share. The article highlights the PMI revision as the main economic update.

feed.title: Verizon Communications: The Case Behind Its $20 Billion Frontier Bet and $25 Billion Buyback
feed.summary: Verizon Communications demonstrated strong performance in Q4, with significant subscriber growth and cost cutting, alongside the $20 billion Frontier acquisition and a new $25 billion share repurchase program. The company aims for substantial free cash flow growth and churn reduction through its convergence strategy, targeting 40 to 50 million fiber passings. While Wall Street has a mixed view, a valuation model suggests considerable upside, indicating the market may be underpricing Verizon's recovery and strategic initiatives.

feed.title: Above the Noise: Investor angst and market endurance
feed.summary: Brian Levitt addresses investor concerns regarding geopolitical tensions and the perceived AI bubble. He advises that historical data shows markets often rebound after geopolitical shocks, and the current US-Iran conflict is unlikely to alter long-term global growth or central bank policy trajectory. Levitt also details that the AI market is experiencing a "reset" in valuation and sentiment, not a collapse, and highlights continued strength in related capital spending and balance sheets.

feed.title: Apple's New MacBook Neo Is Really Cheap (And Powered By An iPhone Chip)
feed.summary: Apple has introduced the MacBook Neo, its most affordable MacBook to date, starting at $599 ($499 for education). This new laptop is powered by the A18 Pro chip, the same processor found in the iPhone 16 Pro, marking the first time an iPhone chip has been used in a Mac. Designed for casual users and those new to macOS, it features a 13-inch Liquid Retina display, Magic Keyboard with Touch ID, up to 512GB of storage, and a 16-hour battery life.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise deploys HPE insights to drive MercedesAMGF1 race readiness, Hewlett Packard Enterprise asserts
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has announced the deployment of its technology with the Mercedes-AMG F1 team to provide data-driven insights. This collaboration aims to enhance precision and performance for race preparations. While specific implementation details were not disclosed, the partnership is expected to give the team a competitive advantage.

feed.title: Teradyne’s 5G Open RAN Push Meets Lofty Valuation Expectations
feed.summary: Teradyne is collaborating with LitePoint, Aethertek, and Metanoia to advance next-generation 5G FR2 Open Radio Units for global Open RAN deployments, with a live demonstration planned for Mobile World Congress 2026. This initiative aligns Teradyne's automated test equipment expertise with emerging 5G use cases, highlighting its position in an expanding market. Investors should note the company's strong short-term price strength but also consider its high valuation, with shares trading significantly above estimated fair value and a P/E ratio higher than the industry average.

feed.title: Here's Why You Should Retain Revvity Stock in Your Portfolio for Now
feed.summary: Revvity, Inc. (RVTY) is well-positioned for growth due to its strong product portfolio, particularly in diagnostics and its expanding Signals Software business. While the company faces near-term challenges like China’s reimbursement reforms and muted organic growth outlook, its strong cash generation, capital allocation discipline, and positive estimate revisions support its Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) status. The article suggests retaining the stock in a portfolio despite potential headwinds.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips (Ondo Tokenized Stock) Analytics
feed.summary: This article provides analytics for ConocoPhillips (Ondo Tokenized Stock) (COPON), focusing on its price dynamics, quarterly returns, and annual price changes in USD. It details price changes and percentages over various periods, from 7 days to 3 years. The current price of COPON is listed at $116.79, with slight negative changes across all measured timeframes.

feed.title: A Look At MarketAxess (MKTX) Valuation After CTO Hire And DirectBooks Technology Integration
feed.summary: MarketAxess Holdings (MKTX) has recently appointed William Quan as CTO and integrated with DirectBooks, driving focus on technology and client workflow efficiency. The stock is currently trading near its narrative fair value, but a DCF model suggests further upside. Investors are encouraged to consider these factors alongside other data-driven opportunities.

feed.title: Arch Capital Group Weighs Record 2025 Results Against Capital Return Potential
feed.summary: Arch Capital Group reported record financial performance for 2025, driven by strong Reinsurance and Mortgage segments, and announced a capital plan that could return almost all generated capital to shareholders if growth opportunities are limited. Despite analysts expecting a decline in earnings over the next three years, the company's valuation appears favorable, trading 57.1% below its estimated fair value. Investors will closely watch how Arch Capital balances reinvestment in its core businesses with potential capital returns, especially given its strong historical stock performance.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Howmet: "This Thing Has No Quit In It"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Howmet Aerospace (NYSE: HWM) as a top-performing S&P 500 stock, noting its over 800% gain in five years and strong aerospace market position. Aristotle Growth Equity Fund's Q4 2025 letter also praised Howmet for its critical role in aerospace, power generation, and transportation, citing its strong growth outlook despite higher valuations. The company benefits from increasing demand in global travel, defense budgets, and industrial gas turbines, with ongoing capacity expansions supporting future growth across its diversified markets.

feed.title: The AI Great Divide: AMD Struggles for Footing as Semiconductor Sector Enters its 'Discerning Phase'
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is facing significant challenges in the semiconductor sector's "discerning phase" of AI investment, characterized by intense scrutiny of execution and competitive positioning. Despite a bullish outlook from UBS, AMD's stock has struggled due to factors like a downward revised price target, weakness in traditional gaming hardware, and U.S. export caps on AI accelerators. While AMD offers a credible alternative to Nvidia, it lacks the full-stack ecosystem dominance, leading to investor skepticism as the market prioritizes proven ROI and integrated solutions.

feed.title: 10 Biggest News Stories of the Week: Honda CR-V Efficiently Outperforms Jeep Grand Cherokee
feed.summary: This article highlights the fuel efficiency of the 2026 Honda CR-V hybrid, which was the most popular news story on Cars.com this past week due to its impressive MPG figures. It also compares the CR-V's efficiency to competitors like the Toyota RAV4 and Hyundai Tucson Hybrid, and discusses the trade-offs in fuel economy and power for the 2026 Jeep Grand Cherokee's new turbocharged four-cylinder engine versus its V-6 option. The piece concludes with a list of the top 10 most-read news stories on Cars.com.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Highlights Texas Pacific Land As The Best Performing S&P 500 Stock for February
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Texas Pacific Land Corporation (NYSE: TPL) as the best-performing S&P 500 stock for February, with a 50.5% gain. The company owns significant land in Texas, leasing it to energy producers and selling water for fracking, making it highly sensitive to crude oil price increases. Cramer also mentioned TPL's long-term potential in building data centers on its land, leveraging natural gas or diesel for power.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Highlights Ciena’s Return to the S&P 500
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Ciena Corporation's return to the S&P 500 after 17 years, attributing its growth to demand for fiber optic equipment from data centers. He praised the company's strong recent performance, noting that the current Ciena is in a much better position than it was in 2000. While acknowledging CIEN's potential, the article suggests that some AI stocks may offer higher and quicker returns.

feed.title: Biotech Can-Fite unlocks $4M to fund drug R&D and clinical trials
feed.summary: Can-Fite BioPharma Ltd. announced it has entered into a definitive agreement for the immediate exercise of warrants, expected to generate approximately $4.0 million in gross proceeds. These funds will be allocated towards research and development, clinical trials, and general corporate purposes. The company will also issue new unregistered warrants as part of the transaction.

feed.title: 5 Large-Cap Fintech Stocks to Buy to Tap Huge Near-Term Upside
feed.summary: The article identifies five large-cap fintech stocks—Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR), Moody's Corp (MCO), LPL Financial Holdings (LPLA), Paychex Inc. (PAYX), and Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY)—poised for significant near-term upside. These companies operate in diverse fintech services and are recommended for investment in 2026 due to their strong Zacks Ranks, growth prospects, and competitive advantages. The article highlights how a low-interest-rate environment benefits fintech, and how AI and machine learning are revolutionizing the sector.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on EPAM: "Things Got Worse for EPAM Specifically When the Company Reported Mid-February"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed EPAM Systems (EPAM) and its significant stock decline, highlighting it as the S&P 500's worst performer in February, down 32.4%. He attributed this drop to the company being an outsourced software developer, negatively impacted by generative AI's ability to perform similar tasks more cheaply. The situation worsened mid-February when EPAM reported good quarterly results but provided light sales guidance, intensifying market fears about AI displacement and causing the stock to fall 70% in one session.

feed.title: Cisco Systems Institutional Holdings Decrease
feed.summary: Smith Chas P & Associates PA Cpas, an institutional investment firm, reduced its stake in Cisco Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:CSCO) by 47.1% during the third quarter, selling 81,586 shares and retaining 91,608 shares valued at $6,268,000. This decrease in institutional ownership is noteworthy as it could indicate a shift in investor sentiment or portfolio strategy, although the specific reasons for the sell-off were not disclosed. Cisco Systems is a significant technology company, and changes in its institutional holdings can influence its stock price and trading volume.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble Insider Sells $917K in Shares
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble CEO Ma. Fatima Francisco sold 5,549 shares worth approximately $917,000 in late February, reducing her stake by about 84%. This significant insider sale has raised concerns about the company's short-term outlook, despite strong fundamentals and positive analyst ratings. Investors are closely monitoring the situation for further developments that could impact the stock's performance.

feed.title: Varenne Capital Partners Reduces Stake in Adobe Inc.
feed.summary: Institutional investor Varenne Capital Partners has reduced its stake in Adobe Inc. by 10.7% in the third quarter, selling 5,634 shares. The firm now holds 47,086 shares valued at approximately $16.61 million, making Adobe the 13th largest position in its portfolio. This move signals a potential shift in sentiment for the software company, with investors watching Adobe's upcoming earnings and its performance against AI-powered competition.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says "I Should Have Pulled the Trigger on Regeneron"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed regret for not investing in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ: REGN), stating he should have added it to his Charitable Trust. He acknowledged that Regeneron, despite being a $750 stock and having a quiet but strong performance, is a better drug stock than others the trust holds, with the exception of Eli Lilly. Cramer noted he lost sight of the company's strong progress and credited a caller for bringing it to his attention.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Edgewell Personal Care, Molson Coors Beverage and Royal Caribbean Group
feed.summary: Edgewell Personal Care (EPC), Molson Coors Beverage (TAP), and Royal Caribbean Group (RCL) are scheduled to trade ex-dividend on March 6, 2026. This means that investors who purchase shares on or after this date will not receive the upcoming quarterly dividends of $0.15 for EPC, $0.48 for TAP, and $1.50 for RCL. Stock prices are expected to adjust downward by the dividend amount when trading opens.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Exponent, Comfort Systems USA and L3Harris Technologies
feed.summary: Exponent Inc. (EXPO), Comfort Systems USA Inc (FIX), and L3Harris Technologies Inc (LHX) are scheduled to trade ex-dividend on March 6, 2026. This article details their respective quarterly dividend payments ($0.31 for EXPO, $0.70 for FIX, and $1.25 for LHX) and the percentage impact on their stock prices when they go ex-dividend. It also provides historical dividend charts and estimated annualized yields for each company.

feed.title: Armis Named a Leader for Second Consecutive Year in 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for CPS Protection Platforms
feed.summary: Armis, a cyber exposure management and security company, has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for CPS Protection Platforms for the second consecutive year. The company's platform, Armis Centrix™, was lauded for its comprehensive approach to managing cyber risk across entire attack surfaces, particularly in securing cyber-physical systems (CPS). This recognition, based on completeness of vision and ability to execute, underlines Armis's commitment to providing unified, proactive security solutions for critical environments.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Insperity, eBay and Terex
feed.summary: Insperity, eBay, and Terex are preparing to trade ex-dividend on March 6, 2026. This means investors need to buy shares before this date to be eligible for their respective upcoming quarterly dividends of $0.60 for Insperity, $0.31 for eBay, and $0.17 for Terex. The article also provides dividend history charts and estimated annualized yields for each company.

feed.title: 65,000-Plus Honda, Acura EVs Recalled for Blank Screens
feed.summary: Honda is recalling over 65,100 of its 2024 Prologue and Acura ZDX electric SUVs due to a software error that can cause the instrument panel and infotainment screens to go blank while driving. This issue increases the risk of a crash as critical information and the rearview camera display become unavailable. Dealers will update the radio control module software for free to fix the problem, with owner notifications planned for April 20.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: TriCo Bancshares, OUTFRONT Media and Northern Trust
feed.summary: TriCo Bancshares (TCBK), OUTFRONT Media Inc (OUT), and Northern Trust Corp (NTRS) are set to trade ex-dividend on March 6, 2026. This means investors need to own shares before this date to receive their respective quarterly dividends of $0.36, $0.30, and $0.80. The article details the dividend yields and historical stability for each company.

feed.title: eBay, Klarna expand embedded resale to six new markets
feed.summary: eBay and Klarna are expanding their direct integration for embedded resale to six new international markets: Australia, Belgium, Canada, Ireland, Poland, and Switzerland. This expansion follows strong engagement in the U.S. and U.K. where the integration allows users to list eligible purchases on eBay directly through the Klarna app, streamlining the reselling process. The partnership aims to facilitate circular commerce and extend product lifecycles by making reselling effortless.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says He Is "Very Worried About Microsoft"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed significant worry about Microsoft (MSFT), stating he's considering selling his stake in the company. His concerns stem from a deceleration in Azure's revenue growth, perceived weak demand for Copilot despite heavy integration, and substantial spending on AI infrastructure with 45% of performance obligations coming from OpenAI, which some on Wall Street no longer see as a reliable customer. Cramer believes Microsoft needs to better explain its narrative, though he notes the stock at 24 times earnings now seems more enticing.

feed.title: Hands-On: Apple’s New MacBook Neo, iPhone 17e and Other New Products
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has introduced several new products, including the $599 MacBook Neo, its first low-end laptop, and the iPhone 17e. The company also updated its MacBook Pro, MacBook Air, Studio Display, and iPad Air, raising prices on some models due to a memory crunch but increasing base storage to offset the cost.
